title: Ozi Advisor Init Routine — sequential per-spoke advisor activation with budget negotiation
what: When a new spoke registers in hub-registry.json, Ozi runs an advisor init sequence. Each advisor is initialized in sequence (not parallel) so budget allocation is informed by what prior advisors already claimed. Each advisor reads the spoke context, selects scout templates from the master catalog, customizes prompts for this spoke's file paths, writes schedule.yaml + coverage_taxonomy.yaml, and negotiates a time budget. At the end, an activation_summary.json is written and surfaced to Wilbur.
why: Without init, advisors must be manually wired per spoke — a bottleneck that prevents fleet expansion. With init, every new spoke gets a personalized, budget-aware advisor stack automatically. The sequential order prevents advisor budget wars: high-priority advisors (Vera, Archie) claim first; lower-priority advisors get what remains.
trigger_conditions: new spoke entry appears in hub-registry.json
re-init: ozi init-advisors --spoke <spoke_id> --reset (clears and re-derives from catalog)

step_1_discover_spoke_context.what: Read CLAUDE.md, WAI-State.json, stack markers, project_type from spoke root
step_1_discover_spoke_context.output: spoke_context object: {project_type, stack, team_size, activation_class, goals}

step_2_compute_fleet_budget.what: Read fleet_budget from hub config (total seconds/week across all advisors on this spoke). Default: 3600s/week.
step_2_compute_fleet_budget.output: remaining_budget_seconds — decremented as each advisor claims allocation

step_3_initialize_advisors_in_sequence.order: Determined by advisor registry priority field. Default order: vera → archie → expediter → historian → jordy → luci → will → clara → mark → sage

per_advisor.a_select_templates: Match spoke_context.activation_class against scout catalog — select scout templates relevant to this spoke type
per_advisor.b_customize_prompts: Replace file path placeholders in templates with this spoke's actual paths
per_advisor.c_negotiate_budget: Advisor reads remaining_budget_seconds, proposes time_request, Ozi grants min(requested, remaining * advisor_priority_fraction)
per_advisor.d_write_schedule: Write {advisor}/schedule.yaml with granted budget and cadence derived from spoke activity level
per_advisor.e_write_taxonomy: Write {advisor}/coverage_taxonomy.yaml derived from selected templates, all initially status=draft

step_5_surface_to_wilbur.path: wilbur/WAI-Spoke/lugs/incoming/ (deliver as a lug type=signal, routed_to=WILBUR)
step_5_surface_to_wilbur.wilbur_reads: fleet coverage_score, budget balance, spoke_context, any dimensions with gap_risk=high

scout_catalog.structure: One subfolder per advisor_id. Each subfolder has template scout JSONs with {{SPOKE_ROOT}}, {{SPOKE_ID}}, {{PROJECT_TYPE}} placeholders.

advisor_init_order_rationale: Vera and Archie run first because they produce the highest-density findings per dollar. Expediter runs third because it feeds the queue that all advisors depend on. Historian runs fourth to inform Ozi of behavioral patterns. Jordy and Luci are tail-end because they are spoke-specific and lower-frequency.
implementation_notes: Implement as a skill file at templates/commands/wai-ozi-advisor-init.md. Ozi dispatches this skill when hub-registry.json changes (hook) or on manual trigger. The skill reads the scout catalog from hub, does template substitution, and writes all files atomically.
